column1: # Triggering-eval cases for the analytics-office bench.
# Format: <expected-bench-skill><TAB><prompt>     ("none" = no bench skill should fire)
# Lines starting with # are ignored. One case per line. Keep prompts realistic and
# phrased near sibling boundaries so the eval actually tests the descriptions.
# --- one positive per skill (phrased near a neighbour to make routing earn it) ---

column2: I just inherited a data warehouse from someone who left. I don't know what's in it or what feeds what. Where do I even start?
My stakeholder asked me to build a dashboard showing our social media follower growth by week. Can you help me scope it?
Before we build anything I need to pin down exactly what "active customer" means - the definition keeps shifting between teams. Lock it down.
Design the star schema for our new sales mart - what is the grain of the fact table and how should the dimensions hang off it?
Here is a SQL view one of my analysts wrote for monthly churn. Can you review it for correctness before we ship it?
I am presenting a 32% retention number to the CFO tomorrow and she is skeptical. Help me get ready for the grilling.
I have finished the analysis. Help me write it up into a findings brief for the stakeholders.
Our churn KPI suddenly jumped from 4% to 11% overnight and leadership is asking why. The number looks wrong - what happened?

column1: # --- intra-bench confusion probes (the no-router bet: the right neighbour must win) ---

column2: The revenue metric reconciles differently in two dashboards. I need one authoritative definition with the edge cases nailed down.
We are about to build the subscription mart. How should I model the SCD on the customer dimension and what grain should the fact be?
Is this query right? It is supposed to match our locked churn contract but the numbers feel off.
The dashboard showed 9% yesterday and 18% today with no code change. Why is today's figure wrong?

column1: # --- kb-reconcile (skill #9: audits the WHOLE record's integrity) + boundary controls ---

column2: Before the board readout I want to be sure our knowledge base is still accurate. Reconcile it against its sources and tell me if anything has drifted.
Our project notes, decisions, and contracts have piled up over months. Is what we wrote down still consistent, or did something go stale or start contradicting itself?
Audit the whole knowledge base before we lean on it for the funding call: does any conclusion contradict the contract or the data it cites?
Review this one inherited SQL view for monthly churn against our locked contract before it ships.
Our churn number spiked from 4% to 11% overnight and looks wrong. Why is this one figure off?

column1: # --- true negatives (no bench skill should fire) ---

column2: Write me a Python function that reverses a singly linked list in place.
What is a good authentic recipe for spaghetti carbonara?
Explain how the TCP three-way handshake works.
I built this churn analysis myself and I'm presenting it to the board tomorrow. Before I stand behind it, what am I assuming that could be wrong?
This revenue figure has been on our dashboard for two years and everyone trusts it. Before I lean on it in the QBR, is it still measuring the right thing?
My findings are validated and the contract is locked. Help me write them up into a board readout.
The number is solid but the CFO will push hard on it next week. Drill me so I can hold up under the grilling.
